scriptId: cs_v4_NOISE_L40_Renovating or fixing up the home
historyId: h_v4_NOISE_L40_Renovating or fixing up the home

goal: Develop a practical DIY renovation plan for the home
constraints: no large crews; specialty tasks only
availability evenings and weekends; short, discrete tasks suitable for two-person team
budget ceiling of about $4,500 for materials and paid help
prioritize low-emission paints and finishes (low/zero-VOC) to minimize fumes
avoid heavy demolition and minimize dust and disruption as much as possible
decisions: Include a contractor vetting and contract checklist in the DIY plan
Decide on trim strategy: simple baseboard vs ornate molding
entities: baseboard and casing options suitable for bungalow style
open: organize fabric swatches and paint chips
draft contractor vetting and contract checklist
evaluate peel-and-stick backsplash durability around the sink area
prepare compatibility/readiness checklist for chosen peel-and-stick backsplash product
draft a short install checklist for the chosen product
evaluate trim scope and finalize whether to include decorative molding
propose two pared-down trim approaches: 'high impact, low fuss' and 'fancier focal details only'
test trim scale with tape samples on walls to preview effect
collect cost/time estimates for trim options
